news.headline: Prime Drink Group closed private placement, raising $99,960 through 100 Units, issuing 588,000 shares and warrants, total of $1,910,235.60.
news.summary: Prime Drink Group Corp. has successfully completed its non-brokered private placement, raising $99,960 through the issuance of 100 Units. Each Unit includes 588,000 common shares and 588,000 common share purchase warrants. The total gross proceeds for the entire Offering amounted to $1,910,235.60. This announcement came on March 27, 2025, in Montreal.

news.headline: SEC closes investigation into Crypto.com, no legal action taken. CEO claims they tried to hinder them, but they persevered. Recently, SEC dropped a series of crypto investigations, including Crypto.com. New SEC leadership adopts a friendlier approach. Crypto.com partners with Trump Media for "Made in America" ETFs.
news.summary: The US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has closed its investigation into Crypto.com with no action taken, marking the end of a seven-month-long probe. The investigation comes after the SEC issued a Wells notice in August, signaling possible legal action against the crypto platform. Crypto.com's CEO, Kris Marszalek, highlighted the challenges faced during the investigation but expressed gratitude for the community's support. The decision to close the investigation was welcomed by the firm's chief legal officer, Nick Lundgren. This move is part of a broader shift in the SEC's approach to crypto, with the current leadership under Mark Uyeda taking a friendlier stance compared to previous actions by former chair Gary Gensler. The SEC has dropped several crypto-related investigations and lawsuits in recent weeks, affecting companies like Coinbase, Consensys, and Robinhood. Additionally, Crypto.com's partnership with Trump Media to launch "Made in America" ETFs later this year was announced. The exchange will provide infrastructure and custody services for the ETFs, which may include tokens like Bitcoin, Ether, Solana, XRP, and Cronos. The article raises key questions regarding the SEC's evolving stance on crypto regulation and enforcement.

Southwest Gas sees rise in 811 requests for marking underground lines, leading to fewer damages; celebrating National Safe Digging Month in April.

The data shows a substantial boost in requests to 811 for marking underground lines, almost reaching a remarkable number. This development is particularly timely as April marks National Safe Digging Month.

Medi-Ops and Southwest Transplant Alliance complete first commercial on-board courier flight with Paragonix KidneyVault, expanding kidney transplant access. #milestone
news.summary: Cutting-edge partnership allows for the transportation of organs over large distances, increasing opportunities for crucial kidney transplants. Recently announced on March 27, 2025, the collaboration between Medi-Ops, a medical logistics group harnessing technology, and Southwest Transplant Alliance (STA) aims to enhance access to life-saving procedures. This development in the medical field comes as a significant advancement, with the potential to positively impact a vast number of patients in need of kidney transplants.

SEC ends investigation into Crypto.com with no action taken after months of attempts to impede the industry, CEO says lawsuit filed.
news.summary: The US Securities and Exchange Commission has concluded its investigation into Crypto.com with no legal action taken. The investigation started in August when the SEC issued a Wells notice to the crypto platform. Despite facing challenges like limited banking access, investors, and auditors, Crypto.com CEO Kris Marszalek stated that the SEC's intent was to hinder the industry. In March, the investigation was officially closed with no action. Marszalek highlighted the resilience and community support as key factors in overcoming the hurdles. In response to the SEC's actions, Crypto.com filed a lawsuit in October, which accused the commission of regulatory overreach in the crypto space. The situation is ongoing, with updates to follow.

Salesforce raises quarterly dividend payout by 4% to $0.42 per share, payable on April 24, 2025.
news.summary: San Francisco - Salesforce, known as the leading AI CRM globally, has disclosed a quarterly cash dividend of $0.42 per share ($0.416 per share in actual terms) as per the declaration by the board of directors. This marks a 4% surge from the dividend of the previous quarter. The dividend will be distributed on April 24, 2025, to shareholders holding the record as of April 10, 2025. Salesforce specializes in assisting organizations, regardless of their scale, in reshaping their operations with AI.

news.headline: CoreWeave IPO priced at $40/share, below range, with weak AI stock demand. Nvidia-backed cloud computing startup faces challenges.
news.summary: The initial public offering of CoreWeave was set at $40 per share, considerably lower than the anticipated range. This cloud-computing startup, supported by Nvidia, is evaluating the market interest for stocks related to artificial intelligence. The pricing indicates a weaker demand for AI stocks as CoreWeave tests the waters with its IPO.

SEC concludes investigation into Crypto.com with no action taken despite previous Wells notice. CEO Kris Marszalek states agency tried to stifle them but they persevered. Lawsuit against SEC filed. Story developing.
news.summary: The US Securities and Exchange Commission has concluded its investigation into Crypto.com, with no legal action taken against the crypto exchange. The investigation began seven months ago after the SEC issued a Wells notice to the platform, indicating its intention to pursue legal action. CEO Kris Marszalek stated that the SEC attempted to hinder Crypto.com by restricting access to various resources. Despite these challenges, Crypto.com persevered and emerged stronger. Marszalek criticized the SEC for allegedly trying to stifle the crypto industry. In response, Crypto.com filed a lawsuit against the SEC, accusing the commission of overstepping its authority and employing a misguided regulatory approach. Further updates on this story will be provided as they unfold.

XTI Aerospace plans public offering to sell common stock and warrants; ticker symbol XTIA.
news.summary: Englewood, Colorado - XTI Aerospace, Inc. (Nasdaq: XTIA) disclosed plans on March 27, 2025, to sell shares of its common stock as part of their initiative in VTOL and powered-lift aircraft solutions. The company, known for its innovative approach, aims to offer the shares along with pre-funded warrants. This move demonstrates XTI’s commitment to furthering their technology and expanding their market presence. Investors and stakeholders are expected to closely monitor this latest development in XTI’s strategic growth plan.

news.headline: Founder of Nikola Motor Company, Trevor Milton pardoned by President Trump; promised to end political weaponization.
news.summary: On March 27, 2025, in Phoenix, President Donald J. Trump made a significant announcement, granting Trevor Milton, the founder of Nikola Corporation, a complete pardon of innocence. This decision comes as a part of President Trump's promise to stop the misuse of politics for personal gain.

news.headline: Chinese-American connection strengthened by YouTuber IShowSpeed with 37M followers, streaming 6-hour live trip in China.
news.summary: On March 27, 2025, an American social media star called "IShowSpeed," boasting 37 million followers on YouTube, broadcasted his visit to China in a continuous live stream lasting six hours. The audience, including international viewers, got a glimpse of China through his perspective, showcasing a nation with amiable locals and a clean environment.

news.headline: "NumbersUSA endorses Fairness for High-Skilled Americans Act (H.R. 2315) for immigration reform in the 119th Congress."
news.summary: In Arlington, Virginia on March 27, 2025, the organization NumbersUSA has officially endorsed the Fairness for High-Skilled Americans Act (H.R. 2315) as part of their proposed solutions for addressing immigration policy in the 119th Congress. This legislation, introduced by Representative Paul Gosar of Arizona's 9th District, is aimed at reforming current immigration policies.

news.headline: BA&D and Belay Investment Group sell fully leased California industrial property for $7.4M.
news.summary: In March 27, 2025, Birtcher Anderson & Davis Associates (BA&D) and Belay Investment Group concluded the sale of Foothill Business Park, a multi-tenant industrial property spanning 30,907 square feet in Irwindale, California. This transaction represents the successful divestment of the property, marking the final sale of this valuable asset.

news.headline: Rockefeller Foundation pledges $100 million to provide nutritious school meals to 100 million children globally. Strengthening food systems and promoting nutrition.
news.summary: At the Nutrition for Growth (N4G) Paris summit, it was revealed that a 5-year initiative will be launched to provide increased support to over twelve countries globally as well as in the United States. The Rockefeller Foundation and its collaborators aim to enhance the food systems of these nations, simultaneously amplifying nutrition standards and encouraging local investment.

news.headline: JAPEX (U.S.) Corp. announces equity commitment to Peoria Resources, LLC as part of plan to acquire operated U.S. assets.
news.summary: On March 27, 2025, JAPEX (U.S.) Corp. (JUS), a subsidiary of Japan Petroleum Exploration Co. Ltd., finalized an equity commitment to Peoria Resources, LLC. This move is in line with JAPEX's strategy to acquire operational control in the United States. The specific financial details and stake acquired were not disclosed in the announcement.

news.headline: QDOBA is offering a BOGO deal for National Burrito Day. Most Gen Z fans won't share their free burrito.
news.summary: A popular fast-casual Mexican restaurant has introduced a new promotion offering "Burrito Insurance" where customers can buy one burrito and get another one for free. This promotion was launched in San Diego on March 27, 2025. The restaurant revealed that a majority of their Gen Z customers who avail the free burrito won't be sharing it with others. This initiative aims to bring more happiness to customers who crave more than one burrito.
